topic: NLP-Based Automation of Systematic Literature Reviews in Computer Science
rqs: What is the comparative time and space complexity of QuickSort, MergeSort, HeapSort, RadixSort, BucketSort, and TimSort versus classic sorts like InsertionSort and BubbleSort in academic and industry contexts?
Which sorting algorithms among QuickSort, MergeSort, HeapSort, RadixSort, BucketSort, and TimSort demonstrate superior stability and scalability for high‑performance and embedded system applications?
In which benchmark suites or open‑source repositories do advanced sorting algorithms outperform traditional ones regarding throughput and latency?
base_query: ("Computer Science Researchers" OR "Software Engineers" OR "Data Mining Practitioners" OR "Systematic Review Authors" OR "Academic Librarians") AND ("NLP Automation Tools" OR "Machine Learning Pipelines" OR "Transformer Based Summarizers" OR "Information Extraction Models" OR "Topic Modeling Techniques") AND ("Manual Screening" OR "Keyword Search" OR "Rule Based Extraction" OR "Traditional Bibliometrics" OR "Human Coding") AND ("Precision" OR "Recall" OR "F1-score" OR "Time Savings" OR "Coverage" OR "Scalability" OR "Usability") AND ("Digital Libraries" OR "Academic Journals" OR "Conference Proceedings" OR "Preprint Repositories" OR "Software Engineering Conferences")
